channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: 2 former Penn State football players facing felony rape, sex charges
item.description: Two former members of the Penn State football program, 19-year-olds Jameial Lyons and Kaveion Keys, are both facing rape charges for an alleged July incident.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panthers name Bryce Young starter vs Broncos following Andy Dalton's car crash
item.description: The Carolina Panthers are going back to 2023 No. 1 overall pick Bryce Young at quarterback following Andy Dalton's car crash on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panthers QB Bryce Young will start against Broncos after Andy Dalton sprains thumb in car crash
item.description: Bryce Young will start Sunday at quarterback for the Carolina Panthers against the Denver Broncos after Andy Dalton sprained the thumb on his right throwing hand during a car crash in Charlotte.
item.dc:creator: Steve Reed, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Washington Commanders rookie QB Jayden Daniels misses practice because of his rib injury
item.description: ASHBURN, Va. (AP) — Jayden Daniels missed Washington Commanders practice Wednesday because of a rib injury, casting further doubt on his availability to play this weekend against Caleb Williams and the Chicago Bears in what's supposed to be a matchup of the top two quarterbacks in the 2024 draft.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tua Tagovailoa practices for the first time since suffering a concussion in Week 2
item.description: Tua Tagovailoa practiced Wednesday for the first time since suffering a concussion in Week 2, and the Dolphins are aiming for him to start Sunday against Arizona.
item.dc:creator: Alanis Thames, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: World Series ticket prices double those of last year
item.description: With New York and Los Angeles facing off in this year's World Series, it's not surprising that ticket prices could give some sticker shock. But even with that knowledge in mind, the Dodgers-Yankees matchup is bringing in some historic prices.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, KTLA 5

item.title: Holy Score: Peering into the past to make sense of the present for sizzling Brigham Young and struggling Utah
item.description: Playcaller Andy Ludwig stepped down Sunday and was replaced by Mike Bajakian, but will the change energize Utah's struggling attack?
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Fernando Valenzuela, Mexican-born pitcher whose feats for Dodgers fueled 'Fernandomania,' dies at 63
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died. He was 63.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dodgers vs. Yankees: A look back at their World Series history
item.description: The teams will square off in the Fall Classic for the 12th time in a star-studded matchup that begins Friday, but it’s their first October showdown since 1981. The Yankees hold an 8-3 advantage.
item.dc:creator: Bill Plunkett, The Mercury News

item.title: Dodgers phenom Fernando Valenzuela dies at 63, just days before former team faces Yankees in World Series
item.description: Fernando Valenzuela, the Mexican-born phenom for the Los Angeles Dodgers who inspired “Fernandomania” while winning the NL Cy Young Award and Rookie of the Year in 1981, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ohtani's historic 50-50 ball sells at auction for nearly $4.4M amid ongoing dispute over ownership
item.description: Shohei Ohtani’s 50th home run ball has sold at auction for nearly $4.4 million, a record high price not just for a baseball, but for any ball in any sport, the auctioneer said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Chiefs acquire three-time All-Pro wide receiver DeAndre Hopkins from the Titans, AP source says
item.description: A person familiar with the deal tells The Associated Press the two-time defending Super Bowl champion Kansas City Chiefs are acquiring three-time All-Pro wide receiver DeAndre Hopkins from the Tennessee Titans.
item.dc:creator: Rob Maaddi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: LeBron James, son Bronny take court together in Lakers’ season opener
item.description: LeBron, 39, and Bronny, 20, become the first father-son duo to play together in the NBA, spending 2½ first-half minutes on the court together against the Timberwolves on Tuesday night at Crypto.com Arena.
item.dc:creator: Khobi Price, The Mercury News

item.title: Fox News Digital Sports NFL power rankings after Week 7 of 2024 NFL season
item.description: To no one's surprise, the Kansas City Chiefs remain No. 1 in our power rankings, but what about the rest of the league? Find now how we rank the 32 teams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-Giants star Victor Cruz discusses state of lowly Big Blue, says clock is winding down on Daniel Jones
item.description: The New York Giants, two years after making the playoffs, are a lowly 2-5, and Daniel Jones' time with the team seems to be approaching an end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ottawa shuts out Utah Hockey Club, 4-0
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was an eight-minute blitz from which the Utah Hockey Club could not receover. Anton Forsberg made 31 saves, Drake Batherson scored his third goal in two games and the Ottawa Senators won their first road game of the season, 4-0 victory over Utah HC Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Flaherty to start World Series Game 1 for Dodgers - Rapid Reaction
item.description: LOS ANGELES - Game 1 of the World Series will have a SoCal feel. Not just because it will be played at Dodger Stadium, but both starting pitchers have area ties.
item.dc:creator: Rahshaun Haylock, KTLA 5

item.title: Transgender golfer Hailey Davidson starts next Q school stage despite 275 female golfers opposing LPGA policy
item.description: Transgender golfer Hailey Davidson teed off for the second stage of qualifying for an LPGA Tour card despite a group of 275 female golfers signing a letter opposing gender policy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jack Flaherty will start for Dodgers in World Series opener against Gerrit Cole and Yankees
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Jack Flaherty will start for the Los Angeles Dodgers in the World Series opener against the New York Yankees and Gerrit Cole on Friday night at Dodger Stadium in a matchup of area natives. Flaherty is from nearby Burbank and attended high school at Harvard-Westlake in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: POLL: MLB wants the Tampa Bay Rays to play in the area if hurricane-damaged Tropicana Field is unfit
item.description: Major League Baseball hopes to find the Tampa Bay Rays a temporary home near their fanbase for the start of the 2025 season if their home field can't be repaired in time. Tropicana Field's fabric dome in St. Petersburg was shredded by Hurricane Milton’s winds.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Diana Taurasi still mulling over decision to retire or play another year
item.description: Diana Taurasi is still mulling over her decision whether to play another season in the WNBA or retire. The 20-year veteran and future Hall of Famer told The Associated Press on Tuesday that she’s going to take her time on deciding what she wants to do. “I don’t take retirement lightly.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PPR Podcast #194: Brodie Stump, Kearny QB
item.description: All Kearny's Brodie Stump does is throw touchdown passes. That's why he's earned a center seat in PPR Podcast #194.
item.dc:creator: Maddy Worley, Fox 5

item.title: USF downs UAB, 35-25, outscoring Blazers 28-6 down the stretch
item.description: TAMPA (usf) - Returning to Raymond James Stadium for the first time in three weeks, South Florida (3-4; 1-2 American) outscored UAB 28-6 down the stretch to notch its first American Athletic Conference win of the season, 35-25, over the Blazers (1-6; 0-4 American) on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Brian Siegrist, USF, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Russell Wilson throws for 2 TDs, runs for another TD in Pittsburgh debut as Steelers rout Jets 37-15
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Russell Wilson sat at the podium, uniform still on, in no rush to move on from a moment he believed would always come. Even as the weeks passed and his aching calf struggled to get better. Even as Justin Fields deftly guided the Pittsburgh Steelers in his place.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers finish off Mets with 10-5 win in NLCS
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani carried the injury-riddled Los Angeles Dodgers to baseball's best record during the regular season. Tommy Edman joined the team in late July and helped them reach the World Series. It's been that kind of year for the big-spending Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Dodgers finish off Mets with 10-5 win in NLCS and advance to face Yankees in World Series
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani carried the injury-riddled Los Angeles Dodgers to baseball's best record during the regular season. Tommy Edman joined the team in late July and helped them reach the World Series. It's been that kind of year for the big-spending Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Patrick Mahomes and Chiefs stay undefeated, beat 49ers 28-18 in Super Bowl rematch
item.description: The formula might look a bit different with the defense and running game carrying the Kansas City Chiefs. But the results are awfully familiar as the two-time defending champions keep knocking off teams — especially the San Francisco 49ers.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jayden Daniels to get further tests Monday after leaving the Commanders' game with a rib injury
item.description: Jayden Daniels will undergo further tests Monday after leaving the Washington Commanders’ game against the Carolina Panthers with a rib injury, coach Dan Quinn said, repeatedly adding he had no further update on the rookie quarterback's status.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Browns QB Watson suffers likely season-ending Achilles injury. Teammates upset some fans cheered
item.description: Deshaun Watson's tumultuous tenure with Cleveland has taken another significant turn. His third season appears to be over.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Santa Monica Ferris wheel to light up in support of Dodgers World Series push
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers are knocking at the door of the World Series, and one of Los Angeles' most iconic landmarks will be decked out in blue to reflect the city's support.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: SDSU'S Sack Master Trey White
item.description: Sacks are such an unpredictable stat in the game of Football. You can be absolutely unlockable as a defender, but if the quarterback gets the ball out of his hands before you arrive, it's all for not in the stat column.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: Real Salt Lake legend reflects on his journey, leadership, and life beyond the pitch
item.description: Real Salt Lake legend and current Vancouver Whitecaps FC standout Damir Kreilach joins Royal Riot co-host Mattey Casey for an exclusive interview, just ahead of his much-anticipated return to America First Field.
item.dc:creator: Mattey Casey, ABC 4

item.title: Kyle Larson opens the third round of NASCAR's playoffs as the title favorite
item.description: Kyle Larson has been lucky of late in Las Vegas, where he opens the third round of NASCAR's playoffs on top of the Cup Series standings and the favorite to win Sunday's race.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Alonso and Mets extend season again, beating Dodgers 12-6 to send NLCS back to LA for Game 6
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Not easy to knock out these New York Mets. Pete Alonso golfed an early three-run homer and the Mets hammered an ineffective Jack Flaherty, extending the National League Championship Series with a 12-6 victory over the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 5 on Friday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KTLA 5
